base_prompt:
<purpose>
    Generate a function for a given function-request. Then call the function with the provided arguments. Then print the result.
</purpose>

<instructions>
    <instruction>Generate only the function requested by the user.</instruction>
    <instruction>Fill in the function body with the appropriate code.</instruction>
    <instruction>Do not include any other text. No commentary, no markdown fences.</instruction>
    <instruction>Write code in python 3.</instruction>
    <instruction>Generate the function, call the function, and print the result.</instruction>
    <instruction>Your code must be immediately executable as-is.</instruction>
</instructions>

<function-request>
    {{function}}
</function-request>

<function-arguments>
    {{arguments}}
</function-arguments>

dynamic_variables.function: def add(a, b): int - add two numbers
def parse_wkt_point(wkt: str) -> tuple[float, float] - parse a WKT POINT string like 'POINT(-122.4 37.8)' and return (lon, lat)

    return a + b  

result = add(5, 7)  
print(result)

    Generate a function for a given function-request. Then call the function with the provided arguments. Then print the result.
</purpose>

<instructions>
    <instruction>Generate only the function requested by the user.</instruction>
    <instruction>Fill in the function body with the appropriate code.</instruction>
    <instruction>Do not include any other text. No commentary, no markdown fences.</instruction>
    <instruction>Write code in python 3.</instruction>
    <instruction>Generate the function, call the function, and print the result.</instruction>
    <instruction>Your code must be immediately executable as-is.</instruction>
</instructions>

<function-request>
    def parse_wkt_point(wkt: str) -> tuple[float, float] - parse a WKT POINT string like 'POINT(-122.4 37.8)' and return (lon, lat)
</function-request>

<function-arguments>
    "POINT(-122.4 37.8)"
</function-arguments>
